Functional Unit Text: Interface modular carpet tile, one square meter floor covering. The functional unit is one square meter of floorcovering. Functional unit 1 m².
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE are presented across several tables (Tables 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13). The product itself does not have a dedicated column; instead, the tables present environmental indicators in rows and life cycle stages in columns. The EPD declares a "Cradle-to-grave" scope (Page 2). The system boundaries explicitly include modules A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport to the factory), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport from gate to site), A5 (Assembly/Installation), B2 (Maintenance), C2 (Transport of waste to local disposal), and C4 (Disposal). Modules B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, C3, and D are explicitly marked as "MND" (Module Not Declared) in Table 5 on page 11. The LCA values are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, AP, EP) as rows and the included life cycle stages (A1-A3, A4, A5, B2, C2, C4) as columns. This structure directly maps the declared stage coverage to the table columns, showing the impact contribution for each specific stage. Footnotes on page 11 clarify the abbreviations for the impact categories. Page 7 notes that "modules B1, B3, B4, and B5 are not declared, so the maintenance (B2) is represented for one year." Page 2 also mentions general limitations regarding exclusions, accuracy, and comparability of EPDs. Indicators for "Total non-renewable primary energy resources" (PENRT) and "Total renewable primary energy resources" (PERT) are not explicitly presented as single row items in the tables.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE is presented in a product-specific table on page 5, titled "Material Composition". The table lists various "Component" categories (Yarn, Primary Backing, Precoat Backing, Stabilization Layer, Secondary Backing) and their corresponding "Material" types, with values shown as "% Mass*". This structure clearly identifies the constituent materials and their proportional mass for the product.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: Interface carpet tiles constructed with CQuestTM Bio feature a non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils. This backing is net carbon negative GWP for cradle-to-gate impacts. The entire carpet tile’s GWP for cradle-to-gate impacts is 2.76 kg CO2eq/m2. Biogenic carbon contained in packaging 0.05 kg CO2. BCRP = Biogenic Carbon Removal from Product; BCEP = Biogenic Carbon Emission from Product; BCRK = Biogenic Carbon Removal from Packaging; BCEK Biogenic Carbon Emission from Packaging.
Fire Data Text: Radiant panel (ASTM E-648) Class 1 Smoke density (ASTM E-662) < 450
End-of-Life and Disposal: The modular aspect of the product allows for easy reuse of the product. The product is intended to be recycled through Interface’s ReEntry process. At end of life, the product should be returned to Interface through Interface’s ReEntry process. ReEntry allows us to reclaim the products we've created and use them as raw materials for new ones. More information can be found in Interface’s ReEntry Guidelines or by contacting Interface at 1.888.733.6873 or via email at reentry@interface.com.
Short Description: CQuest™ Bio Modular Carpet Tile features a non-vinyl bio-composite backing, making it net carbon negative for cradle-to-gate impacts. Designed for commercial use, it offers modular installation and is recyclable through Interface's ReEntry process. It is manufactured in the USA and has a reference service life of 15 years.
Long Description: The CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE is a high-performance modular carpet tile designed for commercial applications. It features an innovative non-vinyl bio-composite backing made from bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, which contributes to its net carbon negative Global Warming Potential (GWP) for cradle-to-gate impacts. The product is constructed with recycled content Nylon yarn and is manufactured in Troup County, Georgia, USA. Its modular design facilitates easy installation and minimizes waste. With a reference service life of 15 years, the carpet tile is also designed for end-of-life recycling through Interface's ReEntry program, supporting a circular economy model by reclaiming materials for new products.

Product Name: CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN
Functional Unit Text:
Interface modular carpet tile, one square meter floor covering
MODULAR CARPET ON CQUESTTM BIO WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN VALUE UNIT Functional unit 1 m² Mass* 4.03 kg *nominal value
LCA Table Context:
The LCA results for CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN are presented across several tables, indicating product-specific data. Tables 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12 provide comprehensive impact assessment, resource use, output flows, and carbon emissions for the product. Additionally, Tables 13, 14, 15, and 16 present Global Warming Potential (GWP) results for various yarn weights of the product, including specific product names that are variants of the modular carpet tile.

The EPD declares a 'Cradle-to-grave' scope. The system boundaries for the main LCA tables (6-12) include the following life cycle stages:
- A1-A3: Product Stage (Raw material supply, Transport to factory, Manufacturing)
- A4: Transport from gate to site
- A5: Assembly/Installation
- B2: Maintenance (represented for one year)
- C2: Transport of waste to local disposal
- C4: Disposal (with four scenarios: C4/1 average EU EoL, C4/2 100% landfill, C4/3 100% incineration, C4/4 100% recycling)
- D: Benefits and Loads Beyond the System Boundary

Life cycle stages B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, and C3 are explicitly marked as 'MND' (Module Not Declared) in Table 5 on page 10, indicating their exclusion from the presented LCA results.

The LCA tables are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, AP, EP, ODP, ADP-fossil, PM, IR, ETP-freshwater, HTP-cancer, HTP-non cancer, LU, RPRe, RPRm, NRPRe, NRPRm, SM, RSF, NRSF, FW, HWD, NHWD, RWD, CRU, MFR, EEE, EET, BCRP, BCEP, BCRK, BCEK) as rows and the life cycle stages (A1-A3, A4, A5, B2, C2, C4/1*, C4/2, C4/3, C4/4, D) as columns. This structure directly maps the declared stage coverage to the table format, showing specific impact values for each included stage or scenario.

Footnotes and assumptions impacting LCA data interpretation include: references to UL PCR part A for all tables, definitions of various impact categories (GWP, ODP, AP, EP, SFP, ADP-fossil, WDP, PM, IR, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, LU, RPRe, RPRm, NRPRe, NRPRM, SM, RSF, NRSF, FW, HWD, NHWD, CRU, MFR, EEE, EET, BCRP, BCEP, BCRK, BCEK), and the specific end-of-life scenarios for C4. The EPD also notes that LCAs do not typically address site-specific environmental impacts of raw material extraction or human health toxicity.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition for CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN is presented in a product-specific table titled 'Material Composition' on page 5. The table lists various components such as Yarn, Primary Backing, Precoat Backing, Stabilization Layer, and Secondary Backing. For each component, the specific material (e.g., Nylon, recycled polyester, bio-based filler) and its nominal percentage by mass (*% Mass*) are provided. This structure clearly identifies the constituent materials and their proportions for the declared product.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: CQuestTM Bio A non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resin, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, is net carbon negative. Interface’s CQuestTM Bio is a non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, which is net carbon negative. GWP-biogenic [kg CO2 eq.] is reported in LCA tables. BCRP = Biogenic Carbon Removal from Product; BCEP = Biogenic Carbon Emission from Product are in Table 12.
Low Toxic Key Text: VOC content - µg/m³ (Page 8, Table 2. Installation into the building (A5)). LCAs do not typically address the site-specific environmental impacts of raw material extraction, nor are they meant to assess human health toxicity. (Page 2, Limitations).
Fire Data Text: Fire Table 18. Fire categories related to this product NAME VALUE Radiant panel (ASTM E-648) Class 1 Smoke density (ASTM E-662) < 450
Density Text: Product weight* 4031 g/m² (Page 4, Table "Technical Requirements") and Mass* 4.03 kg (Page 6, Table "Functional or Declared Unit").
End-of-Life and Disposal: At end of life, the product should be returned to Interface through Interface's ReEntry process. ReEntry allows us to reclaim the products we've created and use them as raw materials for new ones. The modular aspect of the product allows for easy reuse of the product. The product is intended to be reused or recycled through Interface's ReEntry process. Disposal scenarios include: C4/1 average EU EoL scenario (37% landfill, 13% incineration and 50% recycling), C4/2 100% landfill scenario, C4/3 100% incineration scenario, C4/4 100% recycling scenario.
Short Description: CQuest™ Bio Modular Carpet Tile with Solution-Dyed PA6 Yarn is a commercial floor covering product by Interface. It features a non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, making it net carbon negative. Designed for modular installation, it has a reference service life of 15 years and is intended for reuse or recycling through Interface's ReEntry process.
Long Description: CQuest™ Bio Modular Carpet Tile with Solution-Dyed PA6 Yarn is a commercial floor covering product manufactured by Interface, Inc. It is designed for modular installation in commercial buildings. The product features a unique non-vinyl bio-composite backing, CQuest™ Bio, which is made with bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, contributing to its net carbon negative status. The carpet tile uses solution-dyed PA6 (Nylon 6) yarn, with a medium yarn weight of 678 grams per square meter. It has a declared functional unit of one square meter and a reference service life of 15 years. The product is intended for reuse or recycling through Interface's ReEntry process at its end-of-life, aligning with the company's commitment to sustainability and circular economy principles. Manufacturing occurs in Scherpenzeel, Netherlands, and Craigavon, Northern Ireland.

Product Name: SOUND CHOICE+ MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING 4.5 MM LVT
Functional Unit Text:
The functional unit is one square meter of floorcovering
MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING
VALUE UNIT
Declared unit 1 m2
Mass* 6.32 kg/m2
*nominal value
LCA Table Context:
The LCA results for SOUND CHOICE+ MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING 4.5 MM LVT are presented in Tables 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12. These tables are product-specific, as the EPD is dedicated to this single product. The EPD states its scope as "Cradle to gate with options" and covers the entire life cycle from "cradle to grave (modules A to C) and potential benefits and loads beyond the life cycle as Module D".

Table 7 (page 11) explicitly describes the system boundary modules, indicating that A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport from gate to site), A5 (Assembly/Install), B2 (Maintenance), C2 (Transport), C4 (Disposal), and D (Reuse, Recovery, Recycling Potential) are included (marked with 'X'). Modules B1 (Use), B3 (Repair), B4 (Replacement), B5 (Refurbishment), B6 (Building Operational Energy Use During Product Use), B7 (Building Operational Water Use During Product Use), C1 (Deconstruction), and C3 (Waste processing) are marked as "MND" (Module Not Declared), meaning they are excluded from the assessment.

The LCA values in Tables 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12 are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, AP, EP, PERE, FW) as rows and the included life cycle stages (A1-A3, A4, A5, B2, C2, C4, D) as columns. Each column directly represents the impacts or resource use for that specific life cycle stage for the declared product.

A key assumption noted is that "While the warranted service life is 15 years, the maintenance (B2) is represented for one year" (page 6). The EPD also states that "No known flows are deliberately excluded from this EPD" (page 4, 7). Limitations regarding exclusions (e.g., site-specific environmental impacts of raw material extraction, human health toxicity), accuracy of results, and comparability with EPDs from different programs are mentioned on page 2. No credits were taken for recycling of production waste (page 7).
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for SOUND CHOICE+ MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING 4.5 MM LVT is presented in a product-specific table under section 1.7 "Material Composition" on page 5. The table lists various components (e.g., PVC, Plasticizer, Filler) and their respective values in percentage (%) by weight, along with a 'UNIT' column indicating '%'. This structure clearly identifies the constituent materials and their proportions for the product.
Fire Data Text:
Table 13. Fire performance characteristics for Sound Choice+
STANDARD SOUND CHOICE+
EN 13501-1:2018 Bfl – s1
EN ISO 9239-1:2010 (smoke development) 158.90%.min
EN ISO 11925-2:2020 No ignition
End-of-Life and Disposal: At the end of life, the product should be returned to Interface through Interface’s ReEntry process by contacting Interface at +1 888-733-6873 for US. Contact +31 33 277 5812 for the Netherlands. Contact +44 (0) 800 313 4465 for UK. Or contact local customer service through interface.com. Disposal in municipal landfill or commercial incineration facilities is permissible in accordance with local regulations.
Short Description: Modular resilient flooring, 4.5 mm Luxury Vinyl Tile (LVT) from Interface, Inc. Designed for commercial applications, it offers durability and is intended for recycling through Interface's ReEntry process. It has a reference service life of 15 years.
Long Description: This Environmental Product Declaration covers all styles and patterns of Sound Choice+ modular resilient flooring LVT (luxury vinyl tile), manufactured in Seoul, South Korea. It is intended for modular installation in commercial buildings. The product is 4.5 mm thick with a total weight of 6320 g/m2 (6.32 kg/m2). Its material composition includes PVC (27%), Plasticizer (9%), Filler (59%), Glass fiber (1%), Pigment (<1%), Misc. (2%), and Additive & Stabilizer (2%). The product has a reference service life of 15 years, based on its product warranty. It is designed for reuse and recycling through Interface's ReEntry process, with disposal in municipal landfill or commercial incineration facilities also permissible.

Facility Address: Crab Apple Way, Vale Park, Evesham, Worcestershire, WR11 1GP, United Kingdom
Functional Unit Text: The functional unit is one square meter of flooring over a 75-year period
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for "VAN GOGH" are presented in tables alongside other products (Art Select, Da Vinci, Opus Enhance, Opus, Knight Tile, K-Trade). The EPD states a "Cradle-to-Grave" scope, including raw material extraction and processing, transportation, product manufacture, product delivery, installation and use, and product disposal. The life cycle stages explicitly included in the tables are A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, B2, B4, C2, C4. Modules B1, B3, B5, B6, B7 are declared as zero impact and are not included in the tables. Module C1 is also not associated with any impact as the floor is manually deconstructed and is not included. Module D is "Module Not Declared". The tables present results for "VAN GOGH" under specific columns for each life cycle stage (A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, B2, B4, C2, C4) for different impact categories (GWP, ODP, AP, EP, POCP, ADPE, ADPF for CML-IA and GWP, ODP, AP, EP, SFP, FFD for TRACI 2.1) and resource/waste flows (RPRE, RPRM, NRPRE, NRPRM, SM, RSF/NRSF, RE, FW, HWD, NHWD, HLRW, ILLRW, CRU, MR, MER, EE). The results are provided for three different product distribution scenarios: United Kingdom, North America, and Australia. Each scenario has its own set of tables for LCIA results and resource use/waste flows. Footnotes indicate "INA = Indicator not assessed" and "Neg. = Negligible". Biogenic carbon emissions and removals are not declared as vinyl flooring products do not typically contain significant amounts of bio-based materials.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for "VAN GOGH" is presented in Table 9, titled "Average material content for the luxury vinyl flooring products in kg per square meter and percent of total mass." This table is grouped, showing data for all listed products (Art Select, Da Vinci, Knight Tile, K-Trade, Opus, Opus Enhance, Van Gogh). Values are provided in both kg per square meter and as percentages of total mass. The table structure clearly labels the product names in rows and material components (PVC, Filler, Plasticizer, Stabilizer, Other, Pigment, Total) in columns, with two rows for each product: one for mass (kg) and one for percentage (%).
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: Additionally, as vinyl flooring products do not typically contain significant amounts of bio-based materials, biogenic carbon emissions and removals are not declared.
Low Toxic Key Text: Indoor Air Comfort Gold (VOC certification), CDPH/EHLB Standard Method v1.2-2017 (California Section 01350)
Fire Data Text: The Karndean flooring products meet the following fire classification and performance standards: EN 13501-1:2002: Fire classification of construction products and building elements. Classification using test data from reaction to fire tests. All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges achieve a reaction to fire classification of Bfl-s1. ASTM E648: Standard Test Method for Critical Radiant Flux of Floor-Covering Systems using a Radiant Heat Energy Source (also referenced as NFPA 253 and FTM Standard 372). All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges achieve Class 1. AS ISO 9239.1:2003: Reaction to fire tests for floor-coverings. Determination of the burning behavior using a radiant heat source. ASTM E662: Standard Test Method for Specific Optical Density of Smoke Generated by Solid Materials, also referenced as NFPA 258. All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges meet <450 requirement for smoke density.
End-of-Life and Disposal: At end-of-life, the products may be disposed of in a landfill or via incineration.
Short Description: Karndean Designflooring's Van Gogh is a luxury vinyl tile (LVT) flooring product suitable for both commercial and residential interiors. It features a layered structure with a PU protective layer, PVC wear layer, photographic layer, and backing layers containing post-industrial recycled material. The product has a reference service life of 35 years and is certified for indoor air quality.
Long Description: Karndean Designflooring's Van Gogh is a luxury vinyl tile (LVT) flooring product designed for both commercial and residential interior applications. It is available in various designs, formats, and sizes, including tiles and planks. The product is constructed with multiple layers, including a PU protective layer, a clear embossed PVC wear layer, a high-definition photographic layer, and stability and backing layers that incorporate post-industrial recycled material. The primary materials include polyvinyl chloride (PVC), plasticizers, fillers, and various stabilizers and coatings. Manufacturing occurs in China, South Korea, and Taiwan. The product has a reference service life of 35 years and is certified by FloorScore® and Indoor Air Comfort Gold for VOC emissions, ensuring good indoor air quality. At end-of-life, it is assumed to be disposed of in a landfill or via incineration.

Fire Data Text: The Karndean flooring products meet the following fire classification and performance standards: EN 13501-1:2002: Fire classification of construction products and building elements. Classification using test data from reaction to fire tests. All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges achieve a reaction to fire classification of Bfl-s1. ASTM E648: Standard Test Method for Critical Radiant Flux of Floor-Covering Systems using a Radiant Heat Energy Source (also referenced as NFPA 253 and FTM Standard 372). All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges achieve Class 1. AS ISO 9239.1:2003: Reaction to fire tests for floor-coverings. Determination of the burning behavior using a radiant heat source. ASTM E662: Standard Test Method for Specific Optical Density of Smoke Generated by Solid Materials, also referenced as NFPA 258. All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges meet <450 requirement for smoke density.
End-of-Life and Disposal: At end-of-life, the products may be disposed of in a landfill or via incineration.
Short Description: Karndean Designflooring's Van Gogh is a luxury vinyl tile (LVT) flooring product suitable for both commercial and residential interiors. It features a layered structure with a PU protective layer, PVC wear layer, photographic layer, and backing layers containing post-industrial recycled material. The product has a reference service life of 35 years and is certified for indoor air quality.
Long Description: Karndean Designflooring's Van Gogh is a luxury vinyl tile (LVT) flooring product designed for both commercial and residential interior applications. It is available in various designs, formats, and sizes, including tiles and planks. The product is constructed with multiple layers, including a PU protective layer, a clear embossed PVC wear layer, a high-definition photographic layer, and stability and backing layers that incorporate post-industrial recycled material. The primary materials include polyvinyl chloride (PVC), plasticizers, fillers, and various stabilizers and coatings. Manufacturing occurs in China, South Korea, and Taiwan. The product has a reference service life of 35 years and is certified by FloorScore® and Indoor Air Comfort Gold for VOC emissions, ensuring good indoor air quality. At end-of-life, it is assumed to be disposed of in a landfill or via incineration.
_quarantine_reason: DUPLICATE     exact copy of record 27 (Van Gogh, SCS-EPD-06707)

Functional Unit Text: Functional Unit: 1m2 of covered and protected substrate for a period of 60 years (the assumed average lifetime of a building)
LCA Table Context:
The LCA results for product B65W1301 are presented in Table 6, 'LCA Results Using Market Service Life', on page 12. B65W1301 has its own dedicated column in this table, alongside other Acrolon 7300 formulas. The rows represent various environmental impact categories (e.g., Global Warming Potential, Acidification, Eutrophication, Ozone Depletion, Smog Formation). This table provides the 'Total Impact' for B65W1301 across its entire cradle-to-grave lifecycle, including tinting, but does not break down these impacts by individual life cycle stages (e.g., A1-A3, B1-B7, C1-C4). 

The EPD declares that the LCA includes the following life cycle stages as defined by ISO 21930: Product Stage (Stage 1, corresponding to A1-A3), Construction & Design Stage (Stage 2, corresponding to A4-A5), Use & Maintenance Stage (Stage 3, corresponding to B1-B7), and End-Of-Life Stage (Stage 4, corresponding to C1-C4). The system boundary confirms the inclusion of raw material extraction, manufacturing, transportation, application, repaints/maintenance, and disposal. Excluded items are personnel impacts, research and development activities, business travel, secondary packaging, point of sale infrastructure, and the coating applicator.

Table 7, 'Energy, Resource, and Waste Results for Market Life Scenario', on page 13, provides energy, resource, and waste results broken down by life cycle stages (TOTAL, Stage 1, Stage 2, Stage 3, Stage 4). However, this table is presented for a general 'Market Life Scenario' and does not provide product-specific data for B65W1301 in its columns. Therefore, stage-specific LCA results for B65W1301 are not explicitly provided in tabular form for energy, resource, and waste indicators.

Key assumptions and footnotes include: a market-based lifetime of 10 years for the product, with multiple repaints accounted for to meet the 60-year functional unit; global warming values are calculated both including and excluding biogenic carbon; and a 10% over-purchase of coating is stipulated by the reference PCR. No weighting or normalization was applied to the results, and the EPD notes inherent uncertainties in LCA calculations.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition for Acrolon 7300, which includes B65W1301, is described on page 3. The typical composition is shown by % weight for general categories: Solvent (<20%), Resin (20%-30%), and Extender Pigments (0%-50%). Table 2, 'List of Hazardous ingredients in Acrolon 7300 formulas (as mixed)', provides a list of hazardous ingredients with their percentage ranges and CAS numbers, applicable to all Acrolon 7300 formulas, including B65W1301. Product-specific material composition for B65W1301 is not detailed beyond these general categories and hazardous ingredient lists.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: In accordance with the reference PCR, global warming values were calculated and presented both including and excluding biogenic carbon. Biogenic carbon was both included and excluded in the analysis as stipulated by the PCR.
Low Toxic Key Text: VOC determination was done using the federally accepted methods outlined by the EPA in the Federal Register. Other than the materials listed above in Table 2, there are no additional ingredients present which, within the current knowledge of the supplier and in the concentrations applicable, are classified as hazardous to health or the environment and hence require reporting.
End-of-Life and Disposal: Unused product should be taken to an appropriate waste disposal center. See product label for details. Never place unused product down any indoor or outdoor drain.
Short Description: Acrolon 7300 B65W1301 is a high solids acrylic polyurethane coating by Sherwin-Williams. It serves as a durable finish coat over anti-corrosion systems, offering excellent resistance to atmospheric exposures and maintaining gloss and color in corrosive environments. It is tin-free and silica-free.
Long Description: Acrolon 7300 B65W1301 is an architectural coating manufactured by The Sherwin-Williams Company. It is a high solids acrylic polyurethane coating designed as a durable finish coat over high-performance anti-corrosion systems. This tin-free and silica-free aliphatic polyurethane offers excellent resistance to atmospheric exposures, maintaining gloss and color even in highly corrosive environments. It is formulated for interior or exterior architectural substrates such as drywall, stucco, wood, metal, concrete, and masonry, specifically designed to cover and protect metal architectural surfaces like rebar. The product is manufactured globally and has a market-based lifetime of 10 years.
_quarantine_reason: EMPTY         Sherwin-Williams/NSF EPD10557 — 0% LCA fill (likely ISO 21930 stage notation)

Functional Unit Text: 1m² of covered and protected substrate for a period of 60 years (the assumed average lifetime of a building)
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for product N02301W10 are presented in Table 6, titled "LCA Results Using Market Service Life," where N02301W10 has its own dedicated column. This table provides total impact values for various environmental indicators (e.g., GWP Inc Bio Carb, Acidification) across the entire cradle-to-grave lifecycle. The EPD states that LCA results are documented for "Total Impact," "Product Stage (Stage 1)," "Construction & Design Stage (Stage 2)," "Use & Maintenance Stage (Stage 3)," and "End-Of-Life Stage (Stage 4)" as defined by ISO 21930. However, Table 6 only presents the combined "Total Impact" values for N02301W10, without a numerical breakdown into these specific ISO 21930 stages (A1-A3, A4-A5, B1-B7, C1-C4). Figure 2 graphically illustrates the percentage breakdown of impact categories by ISO 21930 stages for the overall Duraplate 301W product, but does not provide numerical values for N02301W10 specifically for each stage. Table 7, titled "Energy, Resource, and Waste Results for Market Life Scenario," presents indicators like Non-Renewable Energy and Consumption of Freshwater. This table includes columns for "TOTAL," "Stage 1" (A1-A3), "Stage 2" (A4-A5), "Stage 3" (B1-B7), and "Stage 4" (C1-C4). However, for all stage-specific columns (Stage 1, Stage 2, Stage 3, Stage 4), the values are consistently "0.00." This contradicts the EPD's system boundary description (Page 7), which includes all stages from raw material extraction to end-of-life, and the interpretation (Page 14), which states that raw materials are the largest impact and transportation is significant. Therefore, while the stage columns are structurally present in Table 7, the numerical data for these stages are presented as zero, rendering them uninformative for N02301W10 or the product system.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for Duraplate 301W, which includes N02301W10, is presented in two sections. Page 2 provides a general typical composition by % weight for Duraplate 301W, listing Solvent (<20%), Resin (30%-50%), and Extender Pigments (0%-45%). Table 2 on page 3 lists specific hazardous ingredients in Duraplate 301 formulas (as mixed), including N02301W10, with their percentage ranges and CAS numbers. This table provides product-specific ingredient details for the Duraplate 301 formulas.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: In accordance with the reference PCR, global warming values were calculated and presented both including and excluding biogenic carbon. For N02301W10, the GWP including biogenic carbon is 4.63 kg CO2 e, and GWP excluding biogenic carbon is also 4.63 kg CO2 e, indicating no biogenic carbon content.
Low Toxic Key Text: VOC Content <200 g/L Determined by EPA VOC Regulatory Calculation. This product contains some VOC, which led to a spike in smog formation during the use phase.
End-of-Life and Disposal: Unused product should be taken to an appropriate waste disposal center. Never place unused product down any indoor or outdoor drain. The applied coating adheres to the substrate where it remains until the substrate is disposed. Any unused coating will be disposed by the customer as well.
Short Description: Duraplate 301W N02301W10 is a two-pack modified epoxy architectural coating. It offers low-temperature curing, surface and humidity tolerance, good chemical and abrasion resistance, and edge-retentive properties. Ideal for on-site applications due to its low solvent content, it's designed to protect metal architectural surfaces.
Long Description: Duraplate 301W N02301W10 is an architectural coating manufactured by The Sherwin-Williams Company. It is a low-temperature curing, surface and humidity tolerant two-pack modified epoxy, designed to cover and protect metal architectural substrates like rebar. This product can be applied without dew point restrictions and over wet steel surfaces, and is suitable for steel prepared by hydroblasting, grit blasting, or mechanical tooling. It exhibits good chemical and abrasion resistance, combined with good edge-retentive properties. Its low solvent content makes it ideal for on-site applications. Classified as a primer/other, it falls under the architectural coatings PCR for decorative or protective paints for interior or exterior architectural substrates.

Functional Unit Text: Functional Unit: 1m2 of covered and protected substrate for a period of 60 years (the assumed average lifetime of a building)
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for M770GLOSS - UD are presented in dedicated columns within tables alongside other SHER-CRYL™ M770 formulas (M770GLOSS - EW, M770SHEEN - UD, M770SHEEN - EW). The EPD states a "Cradle to Grave" scope, which is structurally supported by the tables. The life cycle stages are clearly defined and presented as: Stage 1 (Product Stage, corresponding to A1-A3), Stage 2 (Construction & Design Stage, corresponding to A4-A5), Stage 3 (Use & Maintenance Stage, corresponding to B1-B7), and Stage 4 (End-Of-Life Stage, corresponding to C1-C4). Each table row represents an environmental indicator (e.g., Global Warming Potential, Acidification Potential) or resource/waste category, while columns represent the specific product formulas and sub-columns for each of the four life cycle stages, followed by a 'Total (1-4)' column. This structure clearly shows the contribution of each life cycle stage to the total impact for M770GLOSS - UD under both Market Life and Technical Life scenarios. The EPD notes that no weighting or normalization was applied to the results. Specific resource metrics for individual formulas are available upon request but are not reported in the EPD to maintain simplicity. A spike in smog formation during the use phase is attributed to the product's VOC content.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for SHER-CRYL™ M770 is presented in Table 2: "Composition of SHER-CRYL™ M770 coating by % weight." on page 3. This table provides grouped composition data for "Gloss" and "Sheen" finishes, rather than being specific to M770GLOSS - UD. M770GLOSS - UD is identified as a "Gloss" finish. The table lists components such as Water, Resin/Polymer, Extender Pigments, Pigments (TiO2), and Additives, with their percentage by weight ranges. Table 3: "List of Hazardous ingredients in the SHER-CRYL™ M770 formula." on page 3 also provides a list of hazardous ingredients with their percentage ranges and CAS numbers, applicable to the overall SHER-CRYL™ M770 formula.
Low Toxic Key Text: VOC Content 100-200 g/L Determined by EPA VOC Regulatory Calculation
End-of-Life and Disposal: Unused product should be taken to an appropriate waste disposal center. See product label for details. Never place unused product down any indoor or outdoor drain.
Short Description: Sher-Cryl™ M770GLOSS - UD is a water-based, quick-drying gloss finish or sealercoat for exterior architectural metal surfaces. It provides resistance to moisture and is designed for areas where solvent emissions are undesirable. This low VOC coating is part of the Sher-Cryl™ M770 product range, offering a market life of 10 years.
Long Description: SHER-CRYL™ M770GLOSS - UD is an exterior architectural coating manufactured by The Sherwin-Williams Company. It is a water-based, quick-drying gloss finish or sealercoat designed to cover and protect metal architectural surfaces, such as structural steel. This product is formulated for use in areas where solvent emissions are not desirable and provides resistance to moisture. It falls under the "Architectural Coatings – Exterior Coatings" category, intended for decorative or protective application on various substrates including metal. The product has a market-based lifetime of 10 years and a design lifetime of 5 years. It contains VOCs in the range of 100-200 g/L.
